source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure API keys are rotated every 90 days

input_row.source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure API keys are rotated every 90 days
input_row.description: Replace production API keys with new API keys regularly, every 90 days for example, as a best practice to secure your account.
input_row.rationale: API key rotation can help reduce the impact to your organization if an API key becomes exposed or compromised. If you suspect that a key might have been leaked or compromised, the key must be rotated out of production and deleted. Impact: Any resource that is using an API key that has been rotated out of production will encounter errors until the API key has been updated.
input_row.audit: Console: To find out when an API key was created, complete the following steps: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. From the Menu bar, click Manage > Access(IAM) > API keys . 3. Identify an API key and from the Actions menu, select Details . 4. View the date the API key was created. API:N/A
input_row.remediation: To create new API key, complete the following steps: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. From the Menu bar, click Manage > Access (IAM) > API keys . 3. Click Create an IBM Cloud API key . To rotate an API key, replace an old API key anywhere it is used with the newly created API key. Delete an old API key: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. From the Menu bar, click Manage > Access (IAM) > API keys . 3. Identify the API key you want to delete, and from the Actions menu, select Delete . Default Value: By default, an API key rotation mechanism is not enabled. API key rotation is a manual process References: 1. https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/iam?topic=iam-userapikey
input_row.impact: Any resource that is using an API key that has been rotated out of production will encounter errors until the API key has been updated.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
This control involves verifying the rotation schedule of API keys, which is not directly exposed via an IBM Cloud API or CLI command. The audit instructions provided in the JSON input specify a manual process through the IBM Cloud Console to identify when API keys were last created. Since API keys rotation is a manual task and requires checking individual key creation dates, automation is not feasible. The process requires human interaction and decision-making to ensure compliance with the 90-day rotation policy.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Log in to IBM Cloud Console:**
   - Navigate to https://cloud.ibm.com.

2. **Access API Keys Management:**
   - From the Menu bar, click on **Manage** > **Access (IAM)** > **API keys**.

3. **Identify and Inspect API Keys:**
   - List all user API keys. For each API key, from the **Actions** menu, select **Details**.

4. **Verify Creation Date:**
   - Review the creation date of each API key.

5. **Ensure Compliance:**
   - Check that no active API key is older than 90 days. Any key older than this should be rotated (new key created and old key invalidated).

6. **Update Documentation:**
   - Document the compliance status and update any internal records with the rotation dates and responsible parties to track the API key lifecycle.

**SOURCE:** CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
